God will find a resting place for his ark; if some reject it, the hearts of others will be inclined to receive it. It is nothing new for God’s ark to be in a private house. Christ and his apostles preached from house to house when they could not have public places. Twenty years passed before the people of Israel recognized the need for the ark. During this time the prophet Samuel worked to revive true religion. The few words used are very expressive; and this was one of the most effective revivals of religion which ever took place in Israel.

Israel drew water and poured it out before the Lord, signifying their humiliation and sorrow for sin. They pour out their hearts in repentance before the Lord. They were open and full in their confession, and determined to cast away all their wrongdoings. They made a public confession, We have sinned against the Lord; thus giving glory to God and taking shame to themselves. And if we confess our sins as such, we shall find our God faithful and just to forgive us our sins.

The Philistines invaded Israel. When sinners begin to repent and reform, they must expect that Satan will gather all his strength against them, and set his instruments at work to the utmost, to oppose and discourage them. The Israelites earnestly asked Samuel to pray for them. Oh what a comfort it is to all believers that our great Intercessor above never ceases, is never silent! for he always appears in God’s presence on our behalf. Samuel's sacrifice, without his prayer, would have been an empty shadow. God gave a gracious answer. And Samuel erected a memorial of this victory, to the glory of God and to encourage Israel. Through successive generations, the church of God has had cause to set up Eben-ezers for renewed deliverances; neither outward persecutions nor inward corruptions have prevailed against her, because “hitherto the Lord has helped her:” and he will help, even to the end of the world.

In this great revival of true religion, the ark was neither moved to Shiloh, nor placed with the tabernacle anywhere else. This disregard for the Levitical institutions showed that their typical meaning formed their chief use; and when that was overlooked, they became a lifeless service, not to be compared with repentance, faith, and the love of God and man.
